"Advice to a Friend on Choosing a Mistress" is a letter by Benjamin Franklin dated June 25, 1745, in which Franklin counsels a young man about channeling sexual urges.  Due to its licentious nature the letter was not published in collections of Franklin's papers in the United States during the 19th century.  Federal court decisions from the mid- to late- 20th century cited the document as a reason for overturning obscenity laws.

Text
Franklin begins by advising a young man that a cure for sexual urges is unknown, and the proper solution is to take a wife.  Then, expressing doubts that the intended reader will actually marry, Franklin names several advantages of marriage.  As supplementary advice in case the recipient rejects all previous arguments, Franklin lists eight reasons why an older mistress is preferable to a young one.  Advantages include better conversation, less risk of unwanted pregnancy, and "greater prudence in conducting an intrigue."

According to John Richard Stevens, the unnamed correspondent is a friend of Franklin's named Cadwallader Colden, and it remains unknown whether Franklin was serious or if the letter was ever delivered.  Whether serious or humorous, the letter is frankly sexual:

Censorship
The Mistress letter was not the only document by Franklin that later generations censored.  The bawdy portion of Franklin's writing was accepted during his own era.  Although the Mistress letter was not published during his lifetime, Franklin's public works include an essay called "Fart Proudly".  A passage from his Autobiography describes an unsuccessful attempt to seduce a friend's mistress.  As John Semonche observes in Censoring Sex: A Historical Journey Through American Media, the autobiography was widely read during the 19th century because of its moral lessons, but the passage about the failed seduction was variously altered or deleted entirely.  The Mistress letter was omitted from 19th century publications of Franklin's works, and by some accounts it was singled out for suppression.

This censorship occurred both informally and under law.  The first state to enact obscenity legislation was Vermont in 1821.  During the following decades every state except New Mexico adopted similar laws.  Then the Comstock Act of 1873 made it a federal crime to circulate "obscene, lewd, and/or lascivious" material through the mail.

Although Franklin had mistresses throughout his life (including one still-unknown mistress who bore his only son William Franklin), such circumstances were incompatible with patriotic sensibilities a century afterward.  Amy Beth Werbel opines bluntly:

Citations
By the mid-20th century, United States federal judges were citing the letter in originalist reasoning to overturn obscenity laws.  A Jerome Frank appellate opinion of 1957 named "Advice to a Young Man on Choosing a Mistress" along with "The Speech of Polly Baker" as two examples that would have convicted one of the nation's leading founding fathers on federal obscenity charges if they had been written and mailed under subsequent law.

The most notable of these citations occurred in the United States Supreme Court case, United States v. 12 200-ft. Reels of Film.  In a dissenting opinion, Justice William O. Douglas states:

In popular culture
Franklin appears as a character in Assassin's Creed III (2012), a video game set during the American Revolution. At one point, he has a discussion with the main character about the benefits of older women as sexual partners. Franklin's side of the conversation is an edited version of the Mistress letter.

Red Hot Romance is a genre of literature and film that typically focuses on romantic relationships between characters with sexual tension and explicit content. It is characterized by its passionate and erotic scenes, often featuring graphic descriptions of sexual encounters and taboo subject matter. The origins of Red Hot Romance can be traced back to the early 20th century, when authors such as D.H. Lawrence and Anaïs Nin began writing explicit novels that explored themes of love and sexuality. These works challenged traditional notions of morality and relationships, and paved the way for the emergence of modern erotic literature. During the 1960s and 1970s, the genre gained popularity thanks to the rise of paperback publishing and the sexual revolution. Authors such as Jackie Collins and Judith Krantz became bestsellers, while films like "Last Tango in Paris" and "9 1/2 Weeks" became box office hits. Today, Red Hot Romance remains a popular genre in literature and film. The advent of e-books and online publishing has made it easier for independent authors to write and distribute their work, while streaming platforms like Netflix and Amazon Prime have made it possible for filmmakers to produce and distribute risqué content. Critics of Red Hot Romance argue that it promotes unhealthy attitudes towards sex and relationships, objectifies women, and perpetuates unrealistic expectations. Supporters argue that it empowers women to explore their own desires and challenges traditional gender roles. Regardless of its controversial nature, Red Hot Romance continues to captivate audiences with its steamy love stories and graphic scenes, making it a mainstay of popular culture.

David Macklin is an American rower who achieved great success in international competitions during his career. Born on April 22, 1972, in Rye, New York, he started rowing at the age of 14 at Kent School in Connecticut. Macklin attended Harvard University, where he rowed for the varsity crew team. During his time at Harvard, he won the IRA National Championship in both 1994 and 1995. He also won a gold medal at the 1995 Pan American Games in the men's eight. In 1996, Macklin competed in the Summer Olympics in Atlanta, Georgia, where he won a bronze medal in the men's eight. He went on to win another bronze medal at the 1997 World Rowing Championships in Austria. Macklin's success continued, and he won the gold medal in the men's eight at the 1999 World Rowing Championships in Canada. Macklin retired from rowing in 2000, after competing in the Sydney Olympics. He went on to earn an MBA from Harvard Business School and worked in finance. In 2007, he returned to the sport of rowing as a coach, working with the United States national team. Macklin's achievements in rowing have earned him induction into the National Rowing Hall of Fame. He is also a member of the Kent School Athletic Hall of Fame and the Harvard Athletic Hall of Fame. Macklin continues to be involved in the sport of rowing and is dedicated to mentoring young athletes. In addition to his rowing achievements, Macklin is also an accomplished musician and composer. He has released several albums of original music and has collaborated with other musicians and composers.

Don't Be Tardy was an American reality television show that premiered on April 26, 2012, as Don't Be Tardy for the Wedding on Bravo, making it the first spin-off of The Real Housewives of Atlanta. In May 2021, it was announced that Don't Be Tardy had been canceled after eight seasons.

Background
Don't Be Tardy follows the daily lives of Kim Zolciak, her husband, Kroy Biermann, and their family. The first season was titled Don't Be Tardy for the Wedding, which documented the couple as they prepared for their wedding, as well as the wedding day itself. In November 2012, the series was renewed for a second season, with the series being renamed to its current title. Season 2 premiered on April 16, 2013. The second season encompasses Zolciak as she moves on from The Real Housewives of Atlanta, takes care of her family, constructs her dream home, and manages to live out of the townhouse with the couple's growing family.

Kroy met Kim Zolciak in May 2010 while Zolciak was attending the Dancing Stars of Atlanta charity event to support fellow Real Housewives cast member, Sheree Whitfield. Biermann and  Zolciak started dating and, just three months later, Zolciak was pregnant. On May 31, 2011, Zolciak gave birth to son Kroy Jagger Biermann Jr. This was Biermann's first child and Kim's first son. The couple wed at their previous Atlanta home on November 11, 2011. On March 20, 2012 Kim announced via Twitter that she and Kroy were expecting their second child together. On August 15, 2012, they welcomed their second son, Kash Kade Biermann. It was announced on June 5, 2013, that the couple was expecting their third child together. It was then announced on August 19, 2013, that the couple was expecting a set of twins; the twins are the couples' third and fourth children - Zolciak's fifth and sixth. Zolciak announced that she gave birth to her twins on November 25, 2013. The girl is named Kaia Rose after one of Kroy's nieces, Kiah, and "Psychic Rose" - who Kim is seen visiting throughout her time on The Real Housewives - while her boy, Kane Ren, includes Ren after Zolciak's OBGYN who has delivered 5 out of her 6 children. Rose died on June 5, 2021.

Bravo announced the third season renewal of Don't Be Tardy in April 2014. Season 3 premiered on July 17, 2014. The third season features Kim and Kroy's two new twins (a boy and a girl), their first two children together (both boys), and their two teenagers (adopted by Kroy but fathered in previous relationships of Kim's). The four younger children are all under the age of four. Kroy dealt with a football injury that temporarily suspended his football career. The family has moved into their completed 6,500 square-foot home.

In March 2015, Bravo renewed Don't Be Tardy for a fourth season, which premiered on August 16, 2015 In April 2016, the show was renewed for a fifth season, which premiered on September 14, 2016. In April 2017, the series was renewed for a sixth season, which premiered on October 6, 2017.

In April 2018, the series was renewed for a seventh season which premiered on February 17, 2019.

In July 2019, the series was renewed for an eighth season. The season was due to premiere in July 2020, but was pushed back four months to October 6, 2020.

On May 7, 2021, Bravo cancelled the show after eight seasons.

Dino Dan is a Canadian television series that was created and is directed by J. J. Johnson. The series premiered on TVOKids in Canada on January 4, 2010 and on Nick Jr. in the United States on October 17, 2010. It is produced by Sinking Ship Entertainment, in association with TVOKids, Access, Knowledge Network, and SCN. A sequel of the series, Dino Dana, premiered on Amazon Prime on May 26, 2017. The show premiered on Universal Kids on October 6, 2018, three years after Nick Jr.'s rights to the series expired.

Plot
In this series that combines live action with CGI animation, paleontologist-in-training Dan Henderson and his friends take a journey to a modern-day world where dinosaurs roam freely. There, they have adventures, uncover clues about the past and teach children scientific information ranging from new species to dinosaur vocabulary.

Dino Dan: Trek's Adventures
A follow-up season starring Dan's younger brother, Trek, began airing in 2013. Trek, now age ten, is also able to see dinosaurs and spends his time learning about them as his brother did. There are fifteen new dinosaurs from Albertosaurus to Microraptor in this series. Dan did not have time to do his experiments because he has to help his father, so he asks Trek to do them for him. Although Trek becomes the main character for the episodes, Jason Spevack made periodic appearances as Dan throughout Trek's Adventures, though much of the adult cast remained largely unchanged.

At the beginning of a written work stands the opening sentence or opening line.  The opening line is part or all of the opening sentence that may start the lead paragraph. For older texts the Latin term "incipit" (it begins) is in use for the very first words of the opening sentence.

As in speech, a personal document such as a letter normally starts with a salutation; this, however, tends not to be the case in documents, articles, essays, poetry, lyrics, and general works of fiction and nonfiction. In nonfiction, the opening sentence generally points the reader to the subject under discussion directly in a matter-of-fact style. In journalism, the opening line typically sets out the scope of the article.

In fiction, authors have much liberty in the way they can cast the beginning.
Techniques to hold the reader's attention include keeping the opening sentence to the point, showing attitude, shocking, and being controversial. One of the most famous opening lines, "It was the best of times, it was the worst of times ...", starts a sentence of 118 words that draws the reader in by its contradiction; the first sentence of Yes even contains 477 words. "Call me Ishmael" is an example of a short opening sentence. Formulaic openings are generally eschewed, but expected in certain genres, such as fairy tales beginning "Once upon a time...".

Inspired by the opening, "It was a dark and stormy night...", the annual tongue-in-cheek Bulwer-Lytton Fiction Contest invites entrants to compose "the opening sentence of the worst of all possible novels", and its derivative, the Lyttle Lytton Contest, for its equivalent in brevity.

The opening sentence may sometimes be also used as the title for the work, e.g. Everything I Possess I Carry With Me; papal encyclicals and bulls are titled according to their incipits.

Duke Nukem is a fictional character featured in a popular video game series of the same name. He first made his debut in the 1991 video game, Duke Nukem. The character was created by Scott Miller and developed by Apogee Software and 3D Realms. He is designed as a parody of classic action heroes of the 1980s and 1990s. Duke Nukem is portrayed as a muscular, cigar-chomping gun-toting hero who battles against invading aliens and other enemies. He is always seen wearing sunglasses, a red tank top, blue jeans, and boots. Duke has a rough, gravelly voice and speaks with one-liners, making him one of the most iconic video game characters of all time. Throughout the Duke Nukem game series, Duke's backstory has been fleshed out. He is portrayed as a former Marine commander and a celebrity, with his own talk show and merchandise line. He is also known for his love of women, often seen flirting with strippers and other female characters throughout the series. Duke Nukem's gameplay consists of first-person shooter elements mixed with puzzle-solving and platforming elements. These games have been praised for their innovative gameplay and borrowed elements from classic action games, such as Contra and Doom. Each game has a variety of weapons, from shotguns to rocket launchers, with ammunition scattered throughout the levels. Duke Nukem has been featured in several media adaptations, including comic books, action figures, and a feature film adaptation, which is still in development. He has become an iconic figure in video game culture, often referenced in popular media outside of gaming. In conclusion, Duke Nukem is one of the most recognizable video game characters of all time, known for his humor, brute strength, and love of women. He has cemented his place in pop culture and the video game industry, influencing many future video games and characters.

Omer Madison Kem (1882-1959) was an American chemist and researcher who made significant contributions to the field of agricultural chemistry. Kem was born on January 20, 1882, in Westmoreland County, Virginia. He earned his bachelor's degree from Randolph-Macon College in 1902, and his master's degree from the University of Virginia in 1904. He then went on to earn his PhD in chemistry from the University of Leipzig in Germany in 1910. After earning his PhD, Kem returned to the United States and began his career as a chemist. In 1911, he was appointed as an associate professor of chemistry at the University of Missouri. He remained at the university for the rest of his career, eventually becoming the head of the Department of Agricultural Chemistry. Kem's research focused on agricultural chemistry and soil science. He was particularly interested in the relationship between soil fertility and plant nutrition. He conducted extensive research on fertilizers and soil amendments, and his work helped to increase crop yields and improve soil health. In addition to his research, Kem was also a dedicated teacher and mentor. He was known for his engaging lectures and his commitment to his students. Many of his former students went on to have successful careers in agriculture and other fields. Kem was a member of several professional organizations, including the American Chemical Society and the Soil Science Society of America. He received numerous honors and awards throughout his career, including the Soil Science Award from the American Society of Agronomy in 1951. Kem passed away on February 6, 1959, at the age of 77. His contributions to the field of agricultural chemistry continue to be recognized and appreciated to this day.

Albert Burditt (born 17th August 1945) is an American lawyer and former judge. He is best known for his contributions to the legal community, and his outstanding work in the field of commercial litigation. Albert Burditt began his legal career after graduating with a degree in law from Harvard University in 1970. After completing an internship at a top law firm, he went on to work at several prominent law firms around the United States. Throughout his career, Burditt has been known for his dedication to the field of commercial law. He has represented numerous clients in high-profile commercial disputes, ranging from small businesses to large corporations. In 1992, Burditt was appointed as a judge in the Superior Court of Massachusetts, where he served for ten years. He presided over numerous cases, further cementing his reputation as one of the leading legal minds in the United States. In 2002, he decided to return to private practice, and has since continued to work as a prominent lawyer and arbitrator. Over the years, Burditt has published a number of influential legal articles on topics ranging from arbitration and dispute resolution to commercial law. He is also a renowned speaker at legal conferences and seminars. In recognition of his contributions to the legal industry, Burditt has received several awards and recognitions. In 2010, he was named as one of the "Top 100 Lawyers in the United States" by The National Law Journal. Today, Albert Burditt is widely regarded as one of the most accomplished lawyers in the United States. He continues to work on high-profile cases, and is actively engaged in mentoring young attorneys.

J. Michael Seyfert is a renowned American lawyer and litigator who is recognized for his significant contributions to various fields of law including commercial litigation, intellectual property, antitrust law, and entertainment law. He is best known for his work in representing some of the biggest names in the entertainment industry. Seyfert graduated from the University of Southern California with a Bachelor of Science in Business Administration in 1972 and later graduated from USC School of Law in 1975. He began his legal career in the litigation department of the prestigious law firm, Gibson Dunn & Crutcher. Seyfert is most widely recognized for his work in the entertainment industry, having represented some of the biggest names in Hollywood. This includes representation of Academy Award-winning screenwriter and director Quentin Tarantino, Academy Award-winning filmmaker Martin Scorsese, and Grammy Award-winning musician Keith Urban. In addition to his work in entertainment law, Seyfert has also represented clients in several high-profile antitrust and intellectual property cases. He has been recognized for his work in these areas and has been included in several notable industry publications including The Best Lawyers in America and Southern California Super Lawyers. In addition to his legal practice, Seyfert is also active in the community, having served on the boards of various charitable organizations, including the Saban Free Clinic and the Court Appointed Special Advocates of Los Angeles (CASA). Overall, J. Michael Seyfert is widely regarded as one of the most influential and successful litigators in the legal profession, having built a career representing some of the biggest names in Hollywood and achieving significant successes in high-profile cases across a range of legal areas.

The Hungarian Ground Forces, officially known as the Hungarian Army, is the land-based military component of the Hungarian Defense Force. It is responsible for defending Hungary's territory and its interests both domestically and abroad. The Hungarian Ground Forces consist of approximately 27,000 active-duty personnel, with an additional 11,000 in reserve. The forces include a mix of infantry, armored, and artillery units, as well as combat support and logistics personnel. In recent years, the Hungarian Army has also expanded its special forces capabilities, with the establishment of the Hungarian Special Operations Forces Command in 2012. The Hungarian Army has a long history, dating back to the 19th century when Hungary was part of the Austro-Hungarian Empire. During World War I, Hungarian troops fought on the side of the Central Powers. Following the war, Hungary became a republic and established its own military, which was later incorporated into the armed forces of Nazi Germany during World War II. After World War II, Hungary became a communist state and its military was reorganized to reflect Soviet-style tactics and equipment. However, following the collapse of communism in 1989, the Hungarian Army underwent significant restructuring to meet the demands of modern warfare and international peacekeeping operations. In recent years, the Hungarian Army has been involved in several international missions, including operations in Kosovo, Georgia, and Afghanistan. It has also participated in joint military exercises with other NATO countries and is committed to meeting the alliance's defense spending targets. Despite its relatively small size, the Hungarian Army remains a capable and professional force, equipped with modern weapons and equipment. Its personnel are highly trained and motivated, with a strong sense of national pride and a commitment to defending Hungary's interests.

Antoine Wehenkel (10 February 1910 – 27 February 1992) was a Luxembourgian politician and engineer.  He was a member of the Luxembourg Socialist Workers' Party (LSAP), of which he was President (1970 – 1974).

Wehenkel was first elected to the Chamber of Deputies in 1951, and would continue to be so until 1974.  Wehenkel withdrew from sitting as a deputy to take his place in the government of Pierre Werner between 1964 and 1969, holding the position of Minister for the Budget and the newly created office of  Minister for the National Economy and Energy.  With the LSAP ejected from government by a shift of coalition in 1969, Wehenkel turned to be President of the Chamber, which he remained until 1974, when he retired from politics.

He was a member of the communal council of Luxembourg City between 1951 and 1964.

Suquamish Museum is a cultural institution situated in Suquamish, Washington. The museum was established in 2012 and serves as a center for preserving and promoting the cultural heritage of the Suquamish people, who have lived in the region between the Olympic Peninsula and Puget Sound for thousands of years. The museum is operated and maintained by the Suquamish Tribe, which also runs other cultural and educational initiatives in the area. The Suquamish Museum houses a vast collection of artifacts and artworks that reflect the history, traditions, and beliefs of the Suquamish people. The museum's exhibits cover a wide range of topics, such as basketry, weaving, carving, fishing, hunting, cooking, and other aspects of daily life among the Suquamish. Among the most notable items in the collection are historic canoes, cedar baskets, and woven blankets, all of which have been intricately preserved and cared for by the museum staff. One of the highlights of the Suquamish Museum is the House of Awakened Culture, a gathering space that is used for various cultural events and activities. The building's design pays tribute to the traditional longhouse architecture of the Suquamish people, with wooden beams and columns, and large fire pits in the center. The House of Awakened Culture also features a gallery space that hosts rotating exhibits of contemporary Native art and photography. In addition to its core exhibits and programs, the Suquamish Museum also offers educational and outreach initiatives, such as lectures, workshops, and tours. The museum's staff includes several dedicated educators and cultural experts who are deeply knowledgeable about Suquamish culture and its significance in the region. Together, they are working to promote greater understanding and appreciation of the unique heritage of the Suquamish people, both within their community and beyond.

Amit Jogi is an Indian politician and the son of former Chhattisgarh Chief Minister Ajit Jogi. He was born on March 5, 1977, in Bilaspur, Chhattisgarh. Educational background: Amit Jogi holds a Bachelor of Commerce (B.Com) degree from the University of Delhi. He then went on to pursue a Bachelor of Laws (LL.B) from Delhi University. He completed his Master of Laws (LL.M) from Birmingham University, United Kingdom. Political career: Amit Jogi joined the Indian National Congress in 2003 and worked on the party's campaign during the 2003 Chhattisgarh Assembly Elections. He also supported his father during his campaign for the Chief Minister’s post. In 2008, Amit Jogi became a Member of the Legislative Assembly from Marwahi constituency in Chhattisgarh. He succeeded his father after he resigned from the post following allegations of faking caste certificates. In 2013, Amit Jogi contested the state assembly elections from Marwahi constituency and won. He was appointed as the Member of the Legislative Assembly once again. However, in 2016, he was suspended from the Indian National Congress party due to anti-party activities and indiscipline. He then joined Ajit Jogi's newly formed party, the Janata Congress Chhattisgarh (JCC), and became its National Vice President. Personal life: Amit Jogi is married to Richa Jogi. They have two children together. Controversy: Amit Jogi was accused of submitting a false certificate claiming to be a tribal in order to contest the 2003 Assembly Elections. This led to protests from the tribal community demanding action against him. In 2016, he was arrested for providing false information about his birthplace in an affidavit for the nomination of Marwahi constituency. Conclusion: Amit Jogi has been an active participant in politics since joining the Indian National Congress in 2003. Despite his controversies, he has continued to be a popular figure in the political circles of Chhattisgarh.

Saint Francis School is a private, co-educational Catholic school located in Honolulu, Hawaii. Founded in 1924, the school is named after Saint Francis of Assisi, the patron saint of animals and the environment. The school’s motto is “Pax et Bonum,” which is Latin for “Peace and Goodness.” Saint Francis School offers education from preschool through to twelfth grade, providing a comprehensive and rigorous curriculum that emphasizes faith, academic excellence, and social responsibility. The school is accredited by the Western Association of Schools and Colleges (WASC) and the Hawaii Association of Independent Schools (HAIS). The school’s facilities include a state-of-the-art science lab, a technology center, an art center, and a performance theater. The school also offers a variety of sports and extracurricular activities such as basketball, soccer, volleyball, robotics, and drama. Saint Francis School is proud of its Catholic identity and encourages students to participate in religious activities such as retreats, masses, and service projects. The school promotes the values of compassion, respect, and responsibility, and supports students in their growth as good citizens and leaders. The Saint Francis School community is made up of a diverse student body, faculty, and staff. The school recognizes the importance of diversity and promotes an inclusive learning environment that celebrates different cultures, backgrounds, and beliefs. Throughout its history, Saint Francis School has been committed to providing students with a quality education that prepares them for success in college and beyond. The school’s graduates have gone on to attend prestigious universities and pursue successful careers in a variety of fields.

The Chief of the General Staff of the Army of North Macedonia () is the professional head of the North Macedonian military, responsible for the administration and the operational control.

History
Eleven people served as Chief of the General Staff since the independence of North Macedonia from Yugoslavia. The first Chief was Major general Mitre Arsovski (appointed on 16 March 1992), and the current Chief is Lt. Col. General Vasko Gjurčinovski (appointed on 18 August 2018).

The shortest-serving Chief of the General Staff was General Pande Petrovski (about 3 months), and the longest-serving was Lt. Col. General Miroslav Stojanovski (over 6 years)

Scott Perry is an American politician who serves as the U.S. Representative for Pennsylvania's 10th congressional district. Perry has been serving in this position since 2013 after winning the 2012 elections. Prior to his career in politics, Perry served in the Pennsylvania Army National Guard for 38 years, retiring as a Brigadier General in 2019. Born in 1962 in San Diego, California, Perry grew up in Pennsylvania and attended Northern York County High School before earning a degree in business administration from Pennsylvania State University. After graduating, Perry spent time working in various fields, including as a private business owner and as an employee of the Department of Defense. Perry's political career began in 2006 when he was elected to the Pennsylvania House of Representatives. During his time in the state legislature, Perry was known for his conservative views, including opposition to abortion and support for Second Amendment rights. In 2012, Perry ran for Congress in Pennsylvania's newly-redrawn 4th congressional district, defeating his Democratic opponent by a comfortable margin. During his time in Congress, Perry has continued to promote conservative values, including support for limited government and lower taxes. He is also known for being a strong advocate for veterans' issues, including increased funding for the Department of Veterans Affairs and better access to healthcare for veterans. In addition to his work in Congress, Perry has been involved in a number of community organizations in his home state of Pennsylvania. He is a member of the American Legion, the Veterans of Foreign Wars, and the National Rifle Association, among other groups. Overall, Scott Perry is a well-respected politician who has worked hard to represent the interests of his constituents. While he has faced criticism from some quarters for his conservative views, his dedication to public service and commitment to his constituents continue to make him a popular figure in Pennsylvania politics.

Knowsley Safari Park is a popular tourist attraction and zoological park that is located in the borough of Knowsley, Merseyside, England. The park covers an area of 550 acres and is home to more than 700 animals from over 100 different species. The safari park was opened in 1971 and quickly became one of the most popular destinations in the region. The park is home to a variety of animals such as lions, tigers, meerkats, giraffes, elephants, rhinos, and many more. Visitors can explore the park by car or on foot, and also take part in various activities and events. One of the most popular attractions at Knowsley Safari Park is the monkey jungle, where visitors can enter and interact with the park's troop of baboons. Another popular attraction is the Amur tiger trail, where visitors can observe these majestic creatures in their natural habitat. Knowsley Safari Park is committed to conservation and animal welfare, and as such, it participates in various international breeding programs and supports conservation efforts around the world. The park also hosts various educational programs and events, aimed at raising awareness about the importance of conservation. The safari park offers a variety of dining options, including an on-site restaurant and snack bars throughout the park. There is also a souvenir shop where visitors can purchase gifts and mementos to take home with them. In conclusion, Knowsley Safari Park is a fantastic destination for animal lovers, families, and anyone looking for a fun day out. With its stunning natural setting, vast array of animal species, and commitment to conservation, the park offers an unforgettable experience for visitors of all ages.

Internorm International GmbH is an Austrian brand founded in 1931 that specializes in the manufacture of windows and doors. Its headquarters are located in Traun, Upper Austria. The company is a family business, currently run by the third generation.

Internorm distributes its products through 10 sales offices and more than 1,300 selected sales partners in 22 countries. 55% of its sales are generated through exports.

History 
Internorm started when its founder, Eduard Klinger Sr. established a building and construction workshop in Linz, in 1931. In the following years, Eduard Klinger Sr. expanded his company from a one-man operation to a medium-sized metal construction company. In 1963, seeing the potential of pioneering products made from UPVC (unplasticized polyvinyl chloride), he acquired the first license for the construction of UPVC window frames in Austria.

The company, under the name of "Internorm", started producing UPVC windows in 1966. The first production site was in Traun, the location of the company's headquarters. In 1967, Eduard Klinger Sr. gave each of his three children, Eleonore, Eduard and Helmut a 25% holding in the company. Under the leadership of the second generation, Internorm developed into the market leader for windows and doors in Austria. The rapid growth of the company required a second production site, which Internorm found through the acquisition in 1977 of a local window manufacturer in Sarleinsbach. A third plant was set up in Lannach, Styria, in 1982. In 1988, the first Internorm timber windows were produced in this plant. Today, the plant specializes in Timber/Aluminium products.

In 1997, the 2nd generation left the operations side of the business and joined the supervisory board, each being replaced by one of their children – Anette Klinger, Christian Klinger and Stephan Kubinger – on the executive board. In 2001, Internorm reached a figure of 10 million products sold.

In 2002, Internorm founded the "International Windows Network (IFN)", a new umbrella company to which Internorm belongs to. Other independent and specialist companies emerged alongside Internorm with the goal of developing synergies for sharing knowledge and innovations. The group has around 2,900 employees, with approximately 1,800 of them working at Internorm.

Distribution 

Internorm has its own sales offices in Austria, Germany, Switzerland, Italy, France, Slovenia, Croatia, Hungary, the Czech Republic, Slovakia and Great Britain. Through a two-stage distribution process, the Austrian products are sold in Europe through more than 1,000 sales partners in the mentioned countries, as well as in Luxembourg, Liechtenstein, Belgium, Romania, Poland, Denmark, the Netherlands, Greece and Ireland. All of Internorm's products are produced in Austria through its three production sites.

Product 

The range of products includes UPVC, UPVC/aluminium and timber/aluminium windows as well as house doors made from aluminium and timber/aluminium. Internorm also manufactures sliding, balcony and side entrance doors and a range of accessories such as sun protection, insect repellent and window and door handles.

The products are marketed in four design styles: Studio, Home Pure, Home Soft and Ambiente.

In 2012, Internorm launched a series of technologies marketed under the name "I-tec". These include a new locking system with invisible fitting and higher safety, a ventilation system integrated into the window and an energy-efficient shading system for composite windows. With an intelligent building control, the Internorm products can be conveniently controlled via tablet or smartphone.

Facilities 
The headquarters of "International Window Network (IFN)" and "Internorm International GmbH" are located at the Traun site.  The management, the board of directors and all the administrative sectors are located here. In addition to UPVC and UPVC/aluminium windows, specialized UPVC products (round and angular windows), windows with integrated blinds, aluminium window shutters, aluminium doors and insulating glass are manufactured here.

The plant in Sarleinsbach was opened in 1977. Internorm is the largest employer in this region with around 650 employees. More than 3,000 units of UPVC and UPVC/Aluminium windows are manufactured here per day.

In the Lannach production plant, the manufacture of timber/aluminium windows and timber/aluminium doors takes place. This location is also home to the Internorm sales office for the southern region.

Bank of Springfield Center, formerly known as Prairie Capital Convention Center, is a multi-purpose arena located in Springfield, Illinois. It is owned by the city of Springfield and operated by the Global Spectrum, a subsidiary of Comcast Spectacor. The center features a variety of events including concerts, conferences, sporting events, and theater productions. The center was opened in 1978 and underwent a major renovation in 2018. The renovation included a new LED lighting system, upgraded sound system, updated concourse and concession areas, and new VIP seating. The center boasts a seating capacity of 7,700, making it one of the largest venues for entertainment in the region. Bank of Springfield Center is home to several annual events such as the Illinois High School Association's state basketball tournament and the Illinois State Fair. The center has also hosted concerts by well-known musical artists such as Carrie Underwood, Alice Cooper, and Kid Rock. In addition to its entertainment value, Bank of Springfield Center has also become a center for community events and programs. The center regularly hosts blood drives, job fairs, and fundraising events for charities and non-profit organizations. It also serves as a venue for high school graduations and other local ceremonies. Bank of Springfield Center has been recognized for its sustainable practices by the Illinois Clean Energy Community Foundation, and was awarded the Environmental Achievement Award for the facility's energy efficiency initiatives. The center's commitment to sustainability includes using eco-friendly products, reducing energy usage, and promoting recycling programs. Overall, Bank of Springfield Center plays a significant role in the cultural and economic development of Springfield, welcoming visitors from across the state and beyond, and serving as a hub for local events and activities.

Kyle David Casey (born November 27, 1990) is an American professional basketball player who last played for the Abejas de León of the Mexican LNBP. He played college basketball for Harvard.

High school career
Casey attended Brimmer and May school where he averaged five blocks per game as a senior along with nearly 20 points and 12 rebounds per game. At B&M, he earned several awards, including a Reebok All-America selection, a McDonald's All-America nomination, MVP honors at the 2009 St. Sebastian's Tournament, and was the team MVP for three years.

College career
In four seasons at Harvard, Casey was a two-time All-Ivy League selection and was voted as the league's rookie of the year in 2010. He averaged 10.5 points and 5.5 rebounds per game for his career, and after graduating, he ranked 13th in program history for career points (1,242), 10th for career rebounds (654) and third for career blocks (127). Casey helped the Crimson reach the third round of the NCAA tournament in 2014 after capturing a fourth consecutive Ivy League title. On that span, he led the team with four double-doubles and scored in double-figures 18 times.

Grade controversy
During his initial senior year at Harvard, it was revealed that Casey and teammate Brandyn Curry were among the 125 students involved with the university's 2012 cheating scandal. As a result of the scandal, Casey was forced to sit out of the team's 2012–13 season.

Professional career
After going undrafted in the 2014 NBA draft, Casey joined the Brooklyn Nets for the 2014 NBA Summer League. On August 29, 2014, he signed with Slovenian club Helios Suns Domžale for the 2014–15 season, playing in 32 games and averaging 12.4 points and 7.1 rebounds while shooting 52.8 percent from the field.

On September 16, 2015, Casey signed with the Phoenix Suns. However, he was later waived on October 15 after appearing in one preseason game. On November 2, he was acquired by the Bakersfield Jam of the NBA Development League as an affiliate player of the Suns. On November 13, he made his debut for the Jam in a 104–87 loss to the Santa Cruz Warriors, recording 10 points, six rebounds, four assists, one steal and two blocks in 26 minutes.

On June 29, 2016, Casey returned to Helios Suns Domžale.

On August 23, 2017, Casey was selected by the Wisconsin Herd in the NBA G League expansion draft.

On September 24, 2018, Kyle Casey's NBA G League player rights were traded to the Memphis Hustle who play in Southaven at Landers Center.

Casey spent the 2019–20 season with Earth Friends Tokyo Z of the B.League. He averaged 18.7 points, 11.2 rebounds, 3.9 assists and 1.2 steals per game. On August 11, 2020, Casey signed with the Abejas de León of the LNBP.

Personal life
Casey's mother, Sharon, ran track and field at Texas Southern and in high school alongside Jackie Joyner-Kersee, while his older brother, Randy, played basketball at nearby Wheaton College and Dean College. Casey majored in sociology.

After withdrawing from Harvard in 2012, he worked for a non-profit organization, 3PointFoundation, helping local kids to improve at school and teaching them basketball.

Cindy Shih is a Taiwanese-American software engineer and entrepreneur. She is best known for co-founding the company, Citizen, which provides real-time information about emergency incidents to the public. Early life and education Shih was born in Taipei, Taiwan, but grew up in the United States. She attended Duke University, where she earned a Bachelor of Science in Electrical and Computer Engineering. After graduation, she worked at IBM and Microsoft. Career In 2011, Shih founded a mobile app development company called DesignKILO. Her experience in app development led her to co-found Citizen in 2016. Citizen is a mobile app that uses real-time data from various sources, including emergency services, to inform users of incidents in their vicinity. The app also allows users to post and stream videos of incidents as they unfold. Citizen has been controversial due to concerns over privacy and the potential to incite panic. Shih is also a mentor for the startup accelerator program, 500 Startups. Additionally, she serves on the board of the New York City chapter of Girls Who Code. In 2019, Shih was named to the Forbes 30 Under 30 list in the Enterprise Technology category. Personal life Shih currently lives in New York City. Seomun Market is a traditional market in the city of Daegu, South Korea. It has been in operation since the Joseon Dynasty and is one of the largest and oldest markets in Korea. The market offers a wide range of products such as fabrics, clothing, electronics, and traditional Korean food and snacks. History The market was first established in 1684 during the early Joseon Dynasty and was primarily a place for trading rice and grains. As Daegu grew in importance as a transportation hub for the region, the market also grew in size and importance. During the Korean War, the market served as a primary source of food and supplies for refugees and soldiers. In 1967, the market was officially named Seomun Market and has since undergone multiple renovations to modernize and expand the market's facilities. Today, the market has over 4,000 shops and stalls offering various products and services. Features Seomun Market is divided into several sections, each with its own specialty. The clothing section offers a wide range of textiles such as silk, cotton, and linen, as well as various types of traditional Korean clothing such as hanbok. The food section offers traditional Korean dishes and snacks such as ddeokbokki (spicy rice cakes), mandu (dumplings), and tteok (rice cakes). The market is also known for its electronics section, where visitors can find various types of gadgets and appliances at affordable prices. Additionally, the market has a wide selection of traditional Korean souvenirs such as pottery, calligraphy brushes, and fans. Tourism Seomun Market is a popular tourist destination and attracts visitors from all over the world. Its historical significance, wide range of products, and affordable prices make it an attractive destination for both tourists and locals alike. The market also hosts various events throughout the year such as traditional Korean cultural performances, food festivals, and fashion shows.

Angelo Di Pietro (also known as Angelo D. Pietro) is an Italian-American chef and entrepreneur best known for his signature dish, "Fettuccine Angelo," which has earned him national recognition and multiple culinary awards. Di Pietro was born in Signa, a small town near Florence, Italy, in 1946. He immigrated to the United States in 1958, settling in Hawaii with his family. He worked as a chef in various restaurants before opening his own restaurant in Honolulu, where he started experimenting with his own version of fettuccine alfredo. His recipe featured a unique blend of Parmesan and Romano cheeses, heavy cream, butter, and herbs, resulting in a creamy and flavorful dish. Di Pietro named it "Fettuccine Angelo," and it became an instant hit with his customers. The success of his signature dish led Di Pietro to open multiple Angelo's restaurants in Hawaii, California, and Japan. He also introduced other dishes such as linguine with white clam sauce, eggplant Parmesan, and tiramisu. In addition to his culinary achievements, Di Pietro has won several awards, including the Silver Spoon Award and the American Academy of Hospitality Sciences Five-Star Diamond Award. Despite his success, Di Pietro chose to retire in 2001, closing down his restaurants and confining his cooking skills to his home kitchen. However, his legacy lives on through his famous fettuccine dish, which has been recreated by numerous chefs and food enthusiasts. He is also the author of a cookbook titled "Fettuccine Angelo," which features his recipes and culinary memoirs. Di Pietro's contributions to the culinary world have earned him a place among the best Italian chefs in America. His Fettuccine Angelo dish remains a beloved classic, and his passion for cooking continues to inspire aspiring chefs and food lovers alike.

Fred Alan Wolf (born December 3, 1934) is an American theoretical physicist specializing in quantum physics and the relationship between physics and consciousness. He is a former physics professor at San Diego State University, and has helped to popularize science on the Discovery Channel. He is the author of a number of physics-themed books including Taking the Quantum Leap (1981), The Dreaming Universe (1994), Mind into Matter (2000), and Time Loops and Space Twists (2011).

Wolf was a member in the 1970s, with Jack Sarfatti and others, of the Lawrence Berkeley Laboratory's Fundamental Fysiks Group founded in May 1975 by Elizabeth Rauscher and George Weissmann. His theories about the interrelation of consciousness and quantum physics were described by Newsweek in 2007 as "on the fringes of mainstream science."

Biography
Born into a Jewish family, Wolf's interest in physics began as a child when he viewed a newsreel depicting the world's first atomic explosion. Wolf received his Ph.D. in theoretical physics from UCLA in 1963 and began researching the field of high atmospheric particle behavior following a nuclear explosion.
He has appeared as the resident physicist on the Discovery Channel's The Know Zone, was a participant in the PBS series Closer to Truth, and has appeared on radio talk shows and television shows across the United States and abroad. He also appeared in the films What the Bleep Do We Know!? (2004), The Secret (2006) and Spirit Space (2008). He has lectured on subjects related to quantum physics and consciousness since the 1960s, often under the name Dr. Quantum or Captain Quantum.  He is also featured in the documentary about the Dalai Lama, titled Dalai Lama Renaissance.

His book Taking the Quantum Leap: The New Physics for Nonscientists won a 1982 U.S. National Book Award in Science.

He has taught at San Diego State University, the University of Paris, the Hebrew University of Jerusalem, the University of London, University of Paris at Orsay and Birkbeck College, London.

John Brockenbrough John Brockenbrough is an American entrepreneur, politician, and philanthropist. He was born on December 11, 1964, in Richmond, Virginia, and is the founder and CEO of Richmond-based Avail Vapor, LLC. Brockenbrough is also known for his involvement in Virginia politics and his charitable work. Early life and education Brockenbrough was born and raised in Richmond, Virginia. He attended the Collegiate School and later earned a bachelor's degree in political science from Hampden-Sydney College. Career Brockenbrough started his career in politics, serving as a clerk for Virginia's Senate and House finance committees. He then went on to work for several prominent Virginia politicians, including former Governor George Allen. In 1996, Brockenbrough founded his first company, First Market Bankshares, which he later sold to Union First Market Bank. In 2012, Brockenbrough founded Avail Vapor, which started as a small e-cigarette shop and has since grown into a successful national chain of vapor shops. The company has become a leader in the e-cigarette industry, offering a wide range of e-liquids, vaping devices, and accessories. Philanthropy In addition to his business ventures, Brockenbrough is known for his charitable work. He has donated millions of dollars to various causes, including cancer research, education, and the arts. He is also the founder of the Brockenbrough Charitable Foundation, which provides funding to nonprofit organizations that support children, education, and the environment. Political involvement Brockenbrough has been involved in Virginia politics for many years. He has served on the boards of several political organizations and has been a major donor to local and statewide campaigns. In 2017, he announced his candidacy for the Republican nomination for lieutenant governor of Virginia, but eventually dropped out of the race. Personal life Brockenbrough is married and has three children. He is an avid sportsman, enjoying golf, tennis, and skiing. He is also a collector of classic cars and has a passion for restoring and racing them. Conclusion John Brockenbrough is a successful businessman, philanthropist, and political figure in Virginia. His contributions to the e-cigarette industry and his charitable work have earned him national recognition. Despite his success, Brockenbrough remains committed to his community, continuing to donate his time and resources to help those in need.

Affler is a small municipality located in the state of Rhineland-Palatinate, in western Germany. It is part of the Trier-Saarburg district, which is situated towards the south-western part of the state. The community of Affler has been in existence since early medieval times and has a long history, dating back to the Roman and Celtic periods. It is located in the valley of the Nims river and is surrounded by dense forests, making it an ideal destination for outdoor enthusiasts. One of the most notable features of Affler is the Saint Donatus Church, which is situated at the heart of the village. This beautiful church dates back to the 13th century and is considered to be one of the most significant landmarks in the region. The church is known for its exquisite architecture and impressive collection of art pieces, including antique statues and paintings. Apart from the church, the municipality is also home to several other historical landmarks, including the remains of a Roman aqueduct and a Celtic-Roman settlement. The village is an important archaeological site and attracts visitors from all over the world who are interested in learning about the region's rich history. Affler has a population of approximately 400 people and is known for its friendly community and relaxed atmosphere. The village is surrounded by picturesque countryside and offers visitors several opportunities to explore the outdoors, including hiking, cycling, and fishing. There are also several local restaurants and cafes where visitors can enjoy traditional German cuisine and beverages. In recent years, Affler has emerged as a popular tourist destination and has been developing its infrastructure to cater to the growing number of visitors. The town's tourism board has been working hard to promote the region's natural beauty and cultural heritage, which has led to an increase in the number of tourists visiting the region. In conclusion, Affler is a charming village that is steeped in history and offers visitors a unique glimpse into the rich cultural heritage of the region. With its beautiful countryside, impressive landmarks, and friendly community, Affler is an ideal destination for travelers looking to experience the authentic charm of rural Germany.

Thomas Lewis O'Beirne (1749 – 17 February 1823), was an Anglican bishop, Bishop of Ossory from 1795 to 1798 when he was translated to Meath.

Life
Born in 1749, the eldest son of a Roman Catholic farmer, he was educated at the Catholic College of St Omer before converting to Anglicanism. Ordained a priest in 1773, he was entered on the books of Trinity College, Cambridge as a ten-year man the same year.

His ecclesiastical career began with a college living at Grendon, Northamptonshire 1774–1776, following which he was a naval chaplain under Admiral Howe in 1776. In 1776 he preached in St. Paul's Church in New York City. Later he was Vicar of West Deeping 1779-1783 and chaplain and private secretary to the Duke of Portland 1782–1783.

Returning to his native Ireland he became the incumbent at Templemichael, from which post he ascended to the episcopate: He became Anglican bishop of Ossory in 1795, and in 1798 was transferred to the see of Meath.

He died on 17 February 1823.

KYKR is an American radio station that broadcasts country music listed as the most popular format in most areas. It is based in Beaumont, Texas, and primarily serves the Golden Triangle area, including cities such as Beaumont, Port Arthur, and Orange. KYKR is licensed to King Broadcasting Company, and its frequency is 95.1 FM with an effective radiated power of 100,000 watts. KYKR began broadcasting on October 4, 1970, as a mainstream rock station. However, in 1982, it switched to a country music format, which has remained to this day. This change in format increased the station's popularity, and now it is one of the most listened-to radio stations in the area. In addition to playing traditional and contemporary country music, KYKR has a variety of segments, including news and weather updates, sports coverage, and contests. It also has a highly rated morning show, "The Breakfast Club," which airs on weekdays from 6 to 10 am. Its hosts, Adam Drake and Jenny Law, consistently top the ratings and entertain listeners with their wit, humor, and interviews with local and national celebrities. KYKR has won several awards and recognitions, including the Country Music Association's Medium Market Station of the Year in 1989 and 1990, and the National Association of Broadcasters' Marconi Award for Small Market Station of the Year in 2003. In addition to its on-air presence, KYKR is active in the community, supporting local charities and events. It hosts an annual "KYKR Country Showdown" competition that showcases local talent and provides a platform for aspiring artists. KYKR has a loyal fan base that spans across generations and continues to be one of the most prominent radio stations in the Beaumont area, delivering quality programming and entertainment to its listeners.

Ivan Mozgovenko was a Ukrainian politician and civil activist who played an instrumental role in the formation of the Ukrainian state. Born in 1952 in the town of Donetsk, Mozgovenko spent most of his life fighting for the rights of his people and advocating for the establishment of a democratic government that would represent the interests of all Ukrainians. Mozgovenko's political journey began in the 1980s when he joined the newly formed democratic movement in Ukraine. He worked tirelessly to raise awareness about democracy and human rights, and organize peaceful protests against the repressive Soviet regime. In 1991, Mozgovenko played a key role in the successful bid for Ukraine's independence, and he went on to become one of the country's most prominent and respected politicians. As a member of Ukraine's parliament, Mozgovenko advocated for policies that would promote economic growth, social justice, and democratic governance. He was a vocal opponent of corruption, and he worked tirelessly to strengthen Ukraine's institutions and promote transparency in government. Mozgovenko was also a strong advocate for European integration, and he played a key role in negotiating the Association Agreement between Ukraine and the European Union. Mozgovenko's commitment to democracy and human rights earned him widespread respect and admiration both within Ukraine and abroad. He was a recipient of numerous awards and honors, including the Order of Prince Yaroslav the Wise and the Order of Merit. Mozgovenko's legacy as a champion for democracy and human rights continues to inspire and motivate people around the world. Despite his many accomplishments, Mozgovenko was tragically killed in a car accident in 2015. His death was a great loss to Ukraine and to the global community of democracy activists. Nonetheless, Mozgovenko's commitment to democracy and human rights lives on and inspires countless people around the world to continue the fight for freedom, justice, and equality.

Nadezhda Evstyukhina is a Russian former weightlifter who competed in the 75kg and 75+kg weight categories. She was born on February 17, 1986, in Barnaul, Altai Krai, Russia. She began weightlifting at the age of 12 and quickly rose through the ranks, winning her first national title in 2004. Throughout her career, Evstyukhina won multiple national and international championships and set several world records. One of her most notable accomplishments was winning the gold medal in the snatch and silver in the clean and jerk at the 2010 World Weightlifting Championships. She also won the gold medal at the 2011 European Weightlifting Championships in the 75+kg category. However, Evstyukhina's success was marred by multiple doping incidents. In 2008, she tested positive for methenolone and received a two-year suspension. She was later stripped of her gold medal at the 2010 World Weightlifting Championships after testing positive for the banned substance stanozolol. In 2013, Evstyukhina retired from weightlifting and embarked on a career in politics. She joined the United Russia party and was elected to the State Duma, the lower house of the Russian parliament, in 2016. In this role, she has focused on issues related to sports and youth development. Despite the controversy surrounding her doping offenses, Evstyukhina remains a beloved figure in the weightlifting community for her outstanding performances and contributions to the sport. She continues to inspire young athletes to pursue their dreams and achieve greatness on the world stage.

The Kansas City Southern Depot is a former Kansas City Southern Railway station located at the intersection of Spanish and Port Arthur Streets in Zwolle, Louisiana. Built in 1914, the depot is the only surviving building connected to the railroad in Zwolle. The railroad was built through the Zwolle area in 1896, and the town was founded shortly thereafter; the 1914 depot was a replacement for the town's original station. The railway station was an important shipping center for the town's lumber industry; lumber was Sabine Parish's chief export from the early 1900s through the 1940s, and the railroad made Zwolle one of the two main milling towns in the parish.

The depot was added to the National Register of Historic Places on August 7, 1989.
